---
title: "Stochastic Processes"
author: "John Robin Inston"
date: "2025-12-14"
categories: [Mathematics, Probability]
toc: true
date-modified: "2025-12-14"
title-block-banner: true
---

```{python example-plot}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 7
 
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def simple_symmetric_random_walk(n):
    """
    Generate one realization of a simple symmetric random walk of length n.
    Starts at 0, with steps +1 or -1 with equal probability.
    """
    steps = np.random.choice([-1, 1], size=n)
    walk = np.concatenate([[0], np.cumsum(steps)])
    return walk

# Length of the walk
n = 100

# Generate 10 realizations
walks = [simple_symmetric_random_walk(n) for _ in range(10)]

# Plot the realizations
plt.figure()
for walk in walks:
    plt.plot(walk)

plt.xlabel("Time")
plt.ylabel("Position")
plt.title("10 Realizations of a Simple Symmetric Random Walk")
plt.show()
```

## ✏️ Information

In this topic we study _discrete time Stochastic Processes_, countable sequences of random variables indexed in time.  The material covered in these notes was prepareed during my time working as a teaching assistant under both Prof. Tomoyuki Ichiba and Prof. Vellaisamy Palaniappan for their respective versions of the PSTAT160A Stochastic Processes course at UC Santa Barbara.

## ✍️ Notes

1. Probability Theory
2. Generating Functions
3. [Markov Chains](/posts/prob-markov-chains/index.qmd)
4. Random Walks
5. Branching Processes
6. Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) Methods
7. Poisson Processes

## 📚 Materials

Below are links to documents I have prepared for my past sections as well as a collection of additional exercise sheets covering similar questions to those covered in past assignments and exams.  These documents can also be found in the respective course pages on my GitHub.

### Past Teaching Material

- [PSTAT160A Fall 2025 (Prof. Tomoyuki Ichiba)](https://github.com/johnrobininston/pstat160a-fall-2025/tree/main)
- PSTAT160A Winter 2026 (Prof. Vellaisamy Palaniappan)

## 🙏 Thanks!

If you found any of this material helpful consider buying me a coffee but only if you can afford to!  Thank you for visiting this course page. 😊

<div style="text-align: center;">
  <script type="text/javascript" 
          src="https://cdnjs.buymeacoffee.com/1.0.0/button.prod.min.js" 
          data-name="bmc-button" 
          data-slug="johnrobininston" 
          data-color="#FF5F5F" 
          data-emoji="" 
          data-font="Cookie" 
          data-text="Buy me a coffee! " 
          data-outline-color="#000000" 
          data-font-color="#ffffff" 
          data-coffee-color="#FFDD00">
  </script>
</div>
